description: Should the PM server's agent delegation and Hermes research integration be implemented as extensions to the existing PM server, or as separate microservices?
options: Extend the existing PM server (Bun/Elysia) with new endpoints and logic for agent delegation and Hermes integration
Create separate microservices for agent delegation and Hermes research, communicating with the PM server via API
rationale: Both agent delegation and Hermes research integration are new capabilities. Deciding whether to extend the monolithic PM server or split into microservices impacts maintainability, deployment, and scaling.

description: Which notification bridge services should be used for Discord and Linear integration?
options: Use the existing in-cluster bots/discord-bridge-http and bots/linear-bridge services
Integrate with external SaaS notification services (e.g., Zapier, Pipedream) for Discord and Linear
rationale: There are in-cluster bridge services for Discord and Linear, but external SaaS options could offer faster setup or richer features. The choice affects reliability, cost, and control.

description: How should secrets (e.g., NOUS_API_KEY, Linear tokens) be managed and injected into the pipeline?
options: Use the existing external-secrets operator and CRDs for secret management and injection
Manually provision Kubernetes secrets and mount them directly into pods
rationale: The cluster has external-secrets CRDs and operators, but manual secret management is possible. The approach impacts automation, security, and compliance.

description: What API paradigm should be used for the web frontend to fetch delegation status and pipeline progress?
options: Expose REST endpoints from the PM server for the frontend to consume
Expose a GraphQL API for flexible querying of delegation and pipeline status
rationale: The frontend dashboard needs to display dynamic status data. REST is simple and conventional, while GraphQL offers flexibility for complex queries.

description: How should agent assignments and delegation status be modeled in the backend?
options: Extend the existing task schema to include delegate_id and status fields (CRUD model)
Adopt an event-sourced model to track delegation events and status transitions
rationale: Modeling delegation as simple fields is straightforward, but event sourcing enables richer audit trails and state transitions. The choice affects extensibility and observability.

description: What authentication mechanism should be used for the web frontend dashboard displaying delegation status?
options: Implement session-based authentication with server-side sessions
rationale: The dashboard exposes sensitive assignment data. The authentication mechanism impacts security, scalability, and user experience.

description: How should the frontend dashboard handle pipeline progress and error states?
options: Show real-time progress updates with optimistic UI and inline error banners
Use polling with periodic refresh and modal dialogs for errors
rationale: The dashboard must communicate pipeline status clearly. Real-time updates improve UX but are more complex; polling is simpler but less responsive.

description: Should the Hermes research integration use the in-house Hermes agent or leverage an external research API?
options: Integrate with the in-house Hermes agent (if available in the cluster)
Call an external research API (e.g., OpenAI, NOUS) directly
rationale: The PRD references Hermes research and NOUS_API_KEY. If Hermes is in-cluster, it should be preferred, but direct external API calls may be necessary if not available.

description: What component library or design system should be used for the frontend dashboard?
options: Use an existing design system (e.g., shadcn, Radix) for rapid development
Develop custom components tailored to the Sigma-1 dashboard
rationale: The dashboard is a new UI surface. Using a design system accelerates development and ensures consistency, but custom components may better fit unique requirements.
